name: Peerlist Launchpad
description: Weekly product launch platform inside the Peerlist professional network for tech builders; offers do-follow links and a tech-savvy audience of engineers, designers and founders.

auth.notes: Requires a Peerlist profile (sign up by email or social, then complete your profile). You launch under your account and the do-follow link is tied to a verified profile; launches run in weekly cycles.

best_practices: Peerlist's audience is professional tech builders — frame the launch around craft, the problem it solves and the stack, not hype.
Complete and polish your Peerlist profile before launching; the community trusts launches from people with a real presence on the network.
Write a tagline an engineer or designer would respect: specific, outcome-focused, no buzzwords.
Prepare a strong first comment / maker note explaining why you built it and what's new — it drives early engagement and upvotes.
Launches are weekly, so submit near the start of the cycle to maximise time collecting upvotes on the leaderboard.
Add clean product media (square logo plus a couple of UI shots or a short demo); this design-literate crowd notices polish.
Engage in the comments on launch day — replying to feedback lifts ranking and credibility here.

questions.label: Tagline
questions.help: One specific, outcome-focused line that tech builders will respect.

questions.label: Description
questions.help: What it does, the problem it solves and what's new; speak to a technical audience.

questions.label: Categories
questions.help: Pick the categories that best fit your product.

questions.label: Maker's first comment
questions.help: Why you built it and what's new; posted as the opening comment to spark discussion.

questions.label: Screenshots / media
questions.help: UI screenshots or a short demo; polish matters to this design-literate crowd.
